rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JEANNE T. BUTTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/30/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) No contact information form is on file. LRSF 14.78(A)(3). 2) The "Account Current" box is not checked on the Summary of Account form. 3) Item II(2) of Schedule 1 characterizes the account (5794) as an IRA, but the MFS statement shows this account is non-retirement. The statement shows an IRA account (0988) funded as of 03/23/2018, but this account was not listed an asset on Schedule 1. 4) No supplemental inventory and appraisal has been filed for the property inherited from Gerald Button and reported on Schedule A-2. Probate Code sections 2613 and 2610(a). Also, the reported date of the addition is before the beginning date of this account. 5) Schedule C-1 shows $800 payment on 07/14/2018 for the initial assessment, but the proof of that payment was filed on 05/25/2017. 6) Items 4 and 5 of Schedule D do not show the number of shares. 7) The total amount of losses appears incorrect. 8) The USA Financial statement shows "TOD DTD 03/01/2019" as part of registration of the account (4487). Did the conservator designate any beneficiary for this account? 9) Re Exhibit A: i) the Court's record shows the status hearing on 08/22/2018 was taken off calendar, but the attorney is requesting compensation for the appearance and travel time of 0.9 hours. ii) Please explain the paralegal's work described as "prep decedent and file" on 10/24/2018. iii) The "annuity" mentioned on 02/12/2019 does not appear to be listed as an asset on Schedule 1 or E.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/20 for CIU report to be completed and for proof of payment of assessment fee to be filed if ordered. Additionally, it was noted that the conservator paid himself fees ($2,400 + $18,560); however, there does not appear to be an order authorizing such fees. Errata are not appropriate: additional information must be provided in a supplement to a petition; corrections or materially different information must be provided in an amendment to a petition or a fully amended petition. [CRC 7.3.] An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] The conservator's timesheets are not confidential; they must be filed with his fee declaration (or they could have been part of the petition, which should also have had the accounting attached to it as well as the attorney fee declaration, not all filed separately). The mandatory JC form Summary of Account was not part of the accounting. The accounting begins with $947,533.00, but the I&A showed assets totaling $955,602.34. No Corrected I&A has been filed yet. Assets on hand at the end of the accounting period should identify the specific paintings that make up part of the "miscellaneous personal property." (The account and petition have not been reviewed in detail yet.) The attorney fee declaration must address whether or not the paralegal meets all of the qualifications of Business and Professions Code 6450. The conservatee receives some medical care via the Dept. of Veterans Affairs -- but does not receive any veterans benefits? (Notice should not include a proposed order, as a proposed order is not filed by the fiduciary. Notice of Hearing does not need to be sent to the Court.)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO PLACER COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF ANGELA WAITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/2020. 1) No receipt signed by the general conservators has been filed for the three distributions between 07/01/2020 and 08/27/2020 reported on Schedule C-1. 2) When did the attorney's hourly rate increase from $495 to $525? The attorney's invoice shows the $525 rate in 2019, but the $495 rate was applied on 07/07/2020. 3) In the related matter of the conservatee's deceased spouse (PCN-19-302659), the attorney's hourly rate was $495 through 03/14/2020. Why was the higher rate applied in this matter? 4) Is the paralegal's rate ($250-280) reasonable for all the services described in the attorney's invoice?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORA MAE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/20. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] Notwithstanding LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G., the petition was reviewed online as a one-time courtesy. Courtesy copies must be provided at least 7 Court days prior to the continued hearing. If a sister survived, item 6f of the petition is not the first box that applies. If second generation or more distant heirs survived, the deceased ancestor through whom they take shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] (Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die?) Item 3g(2)(b) of the petition should have been checked, if the sole heir declined to claim her priority and nominated her son; a nephew does not automatically have priority but no declination or nomination was filed. Item 5b of the petition must be answered. No part of item 7b of the petition should be answered if item 7a is checked. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the pettion) is missing from item 8 of the petition. The Duties and Liabilities is missing the petitioner's telephone number. A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the supplement must also be provided to the assigned Examiner at least 7 Court days before 12/23/20.
